subsection.title: Basic antisymmetry
subsection.p: Swapping two factors changes the sign.

subsection.title: Repeated factors vanish
subsection.p: Any wedge product with a repeated differential is zero.

subsection.title: Positive volume order
subsection.p: \boxed{dx\wedge dy\wedge dz} is the positive volume form in the usual orientation of \mathbb R^3.
Cyclic orders are positive: \boxed{dx\wedge dy\wedge dz=dy\wedge dz\wedge dx=dz\wedge dx\wedge dy.}
Reversed cyclic orders are negative: \boxed{dx\wedge dz\wedge dy=dy\wedge dx\wedge dz=dz\wedge dy\wedge dx=-dx\wedge dy\wedge dz.}

subsection.title: Distributive rules
subsection.p: Functions can be moved through wedge products: \boxed{(f\alpha)\wedge\beta=f(\alpha\wedge\beta).}

subsection.title: Graded sign rule
subsection.p: If \alpha is a k-form and \beta is an \ell-form, then \boxed{\alpha\wedge\beta=(-1)^{k\ell}\beta\wedge\alpha.}

subsection.title: Two 1-forms in the plane
subsection.p: This is the determinant: \boxed{ad-bc=\det\begin{pmatrix} a&c\\ b&d \end{pmatrix}.}

subsection.title: Two 1-forms in space
subsection.p: The coefficients are the components of \boxed{\mathbf a\times\mathbf b.}

subsection.title: Three 1-forms in space
subsection.p: Then \boxed{\alpha\wedge\beta\wedge\gamma=\det\begin{pmatrix} a_1&b_1&c_1\\ a_2&b_2&c_2\\ a_3&b_3&c_3 \end{pmatrix} dx\wedge dy\wedge dz.}

warning.title: Common warnings
warning.p: The wedge product is not ordinary multiplication.
In \mathbb R^3, every 4-form is zero because there are only three coordinate directions.
